pipeline.article_content:
Adidas AG stock (DE000A1EWWW0): turnaround story gains pace after strong first quarter
25.05.2026 - 07:54:21 | ad-hoc-news.deAdidas AG has lifted its 2026 outlook after a better-than-expected first quarter 2026, extending the turnaround that began in 2024 and drawing fresh investor attention to the German sportswear group’s Xetra-listed shares.
Adidas AG reported a strong start to 2026 and raised its full-year outlook after first-quarter results beat market expectations, underscoring the momentum of its multi-year turnaround plan, according to a company release published on 05/02/2026 and coverage by Reuters as of 05/02/2026. The German sportswear group highlighted improving demand in key regions and continued progress in cleaning up excess inventory, a key issue that weighed on performance in previous years.
The company said first-quarter 2026 revenue grew at a mid?single?digit rate in currency-neutral terms, while operating profit improved significantly versus the prior year period, helped by better pricing and a more favorable product mix, according to its Q1 2026 statement dated 05/02/2026 on the investor relations site Adidas IR as of 05/02/2026. Management also pointed to stronger sell-through in performance footwear and classic lifestyle franchises as supporting the upgraded guidance for 2026.
As of: 25.05.2026
By the editorial team – specialized in equity coverage.
At a glance
- Name: Adidas AG
- Sector/industry: Sportswear and athletic footwear
- Headquarters/country: Herzogenaurach, Germany
- Core markets: Europe, North America, Greater China
- Key revenue drivers: Performance footwear, apparel, lifestyle sneakers
- Home exchange/listing venue: Xetra (ticker: ADS)
- Trading currency: Euro (EUR)
Adidas AG: core business model
Adidas AG is one of the world’s largest suppliers of sportswear and athletic footwear, operating a portfolio of performance and lifestyle brands that target both professional athletes and everyday consumers. The group generates revenue through wholesale relationships with sporting goods chains and fashion retailers, as well as via its own branded stores and e?commerce platforms, according to its 2024 annual report published on 03/13/2025 on the investor relations website Adidas IR as of 03/13/2025. This omnichannel approach allows the company to balance volume-oriented wholesale sales with higher-margin direct-to-consumer transactions.
The company’s core strategy centers on performance innovation in key sports such as running, football (soccer), basketball, and training, combined with collaborations in the fashion and lifestyle segment. Adidas develops proprietary footwear cushioning systems, upper materials, and design concepts that aim to improve athletic performance, and it partners with high-profile athletes, clubs, and national teams to showcase its products on global stages. At the same time, collaborations with designers and celebrities seek to keep the brand visible in lifestyle and streetwear categories, helping to broaden its appeal beyond purely athletic use, as described in the 2024 annual report published on 03/13/2025 on the company’s website Adidas IR as of 03/13/2025.
Adidas also emphasizes sustainability and circularity as part of its business model, aiming to reduce the environmental footprint of its products and operations. The group has articulated targets for using more recycled materials and lowering greenhouse gas emissions across the value chain, according to its sustainability report for 2024 released on 04/10/2025 on the corporate site Adidas sustainability report as of 04/10/2025. These initiatives intersect with consumer trends, particularly in Europe and North America, where environmental considerations play an increasing role in purchasing decisions for apparel and footwear.
Main revenue and product drivers for Adidas AG
Revenue at Adidas AG is driven primarily by footwear, followed by apparel and accessories. In 2024, footwear accounted for the largest share of sales, with running, training, and football lines among the key categories, according to the 2024 annual report published on 03/13/2025 on the investor relations site Adidas IR as of 03/13/2025. Within footwear, the company benefits from both high-performance models designed for athletes and lifestyle silhouettes derived from archive designs, such as classic court and terrace-inspired shoes that have gained popularity in recent fashion cycles.
Apparel is the second major contributor, encompassing performance gear for sports and training as well as casual and athleisure clothing. Replica jerseys and fan products linked to major football clubs and national teams remain important seasonal drivers, especially in years with major international tournaments. Accessories such as bags, socks, and balls add incremental revenue and often carry higher margin characteristics. In 2024, the company reported that direct-to-consumer channels, including e?commerce and company-owned stores, continued to grow faster than wholesale, expanding their share of overall sales, according to the 2024 annual report dated 03/13/2025 on the Adidas investor portal Adidas IR as of 03/13/2025.
Geographically, Europe, North America, and Greater China are the most relevant regions for Adidas AG. The company has been working to regain momentum in North America, where competition from US-based peers is intense, while also rebuilding its position in China after a period of softer demand. In 2024, the company noted a return to growth in China and improving trends in North America, which set the stage for the stronger first quarter of 2026, according to management commentary in the 2024 annual report published on 03/13/2025 on the investor relations site Adidas IR as of 03/13/2025. The upcoming sports calendar, including global tournaments and league seasons, is expected to influence demand patterns across these core markets.
Official source
